engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 208 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 208 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level diesel for early Mk1 208, robust but prone to EGR issues at higher mileage.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causing limp mode (60k+ miles)
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use
Injector seal leaks leading to fuel odour
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV4TED4 Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Modern BlueHDi replacement for DV6, featuring SCR/AdBlue for Euro 6 compliance and improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: Active, Allure, GT Line, GT, Signature, 1.5 BlueHDi 100
commonFailures: AdBlue injector faults triggering warning lights (40k+ miles)
EGR cooler carbon buildup affecting performance
Turbo actuator wear on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5RD Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Proven workhorse diesel across Mk1 208 range, widely used but requires DPF maintenance for longevity.
compatibleVariants: Active, Allure, GT Line, Signature, 1.6 HDi 92, 1.6 HDi 115, 1.6 BlueHDi 100
commonFailures: DPF saturation from urban driving causing power loss
EGR valve sticking leading to rough idle
Turbo oil feed pipe leaks on pre-2016 units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6TED4 Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Most common PureTech code in 208; wet belt design requires monitoring to prevent catastrophic failure.
compatibleVariants: Active, Allure, Signature, GT Line, 1.2 PureTech 100, HNK code
commonFailures: Wet timing belt degradation causing oil contamination (50k-80k miles)
Belt debris blocking oil pickup leading to bearing failure
Piston ring wear causing excessive oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADT Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Higher-output PureTech variant for GT Line and GT trims, sharing wet belt architecture with EB2ADT.
compatibleVariants: GT Line, GT, Signature, 1.2 PureTech 130, HNS code
commonFailures: Wet belt failure risk identical to EB2ADT (50k-80k miles)
Turbo wastegate sticking on high-mileage units
Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2DTS Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Lower-output PureTech for entry-level trims; same wet belt design but less stress on components.
compatibleVariants: Access, Active, Style, 1.2 PureTech 82, HMP code
commonFailures: Wet belt degradation (slightly later onset than 100hp variants)
Oil consumption from piston ring wear (70k+ miles)
Camshaft position sensor faults triggering management light
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2FA Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Turbocharged performance unit for THP and GTi models; powerful but requires specialist maintenance.
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator failure causing boost loss (60k+ miles)

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear leading to hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6DT Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Uprated THP variant for GTi by Peugeot Sport; specialist rebuilds recommended for performance applications.
compatibleVariants: GTi, GTi by Peugeot Sport, 1.6 THP 173, 5GZ code
commonFailures: Turbo bearing failure under sustained high load (50k+ miles)
Piston ring land cracking on remapped units
Oil pump pickup screen clogging from carbon debris
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6FDT Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated predecessor to PureTech; simpler design but timing chain requires inspection.
compatibleVariants: Access, Active, Allure, 1.2 VTi 82, HMZ code
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling noise (70k+ miles)
Variable valve timing solenoid faults affecting idle
Coolant thermostat failure leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP3F Peugeot 208 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Hybrid
entries.history: Mild-hybrid system pairing PureTech engine with 48V starter-generator for improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: GT, GT Line, 1.2 Hybrid 100, e-DSC6 transmission
commonFailures: 48V battery degradation affecting stop-start function (60k+ miles)

entries.title: generator belt tensioner wear causing noise
entries.cta: Get quotes for HNR Peugeot 208 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 208 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: PureTech Wet Belt Failure - Why EB2 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 208 Active, Allure, GT Line, Signature (2014-2024, EB2ADT/EB2DTS/EB2FA)
problems.rootCause: The wet timing belt degrades prematurely, shedding debris that blocks the oil pickup strainer, starving bearings of lubrication and causing catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wet belt, tensioner, oil pump strainer clean
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before debris enters oil system; prevents major failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove oil pan, clean pickup, replace bearings if undamaged
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if damage is minimal; risky if debris has circulated

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EB2 engine with upgraded belt system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; addresses root cause with updated components

problems.recommendation: If your PureTech is under 60k miles with no oil pressure warnings, a preventative belt replacement offers excellent value. However, once debris has entered the oil system, replacement with a rebuilt unit featuring an upgraded belt kit is the only reliable long-term solution. On a 208 worth £4,000-£8,000, a £1,700-£2,400 rebuilt engine often makes more financial sense than risking a second failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2 PureTech engine prices for your Peugeot 208

problems.h4: EP6 THP Turbo Failure - Boost Loss & Wastegate Issues
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 208 GT Line THP, GTi (2012-2019, EP6DT/EP6FDT)
problems.rootCause: The turbo wastegate actuator sticks or the turbo bearing seals fail, causing boost loss, excessive smoke, or oil consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator, recalibrate boost control
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable only if turbo internals are undamaged; temporary fix on high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip turbo, replace bearings/seals, balance rotating assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EP6 with upgraded turbo kit, high-pressure fuel pump check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage THP; addresses multiple failure points in one solution

problems.recommendation: For GTi or THP owners experiencing boost loss, start with a diagnostic to confirm whether the actuator or turbo internals are at fault. If the engine has over 70k miles, investing in a rebuilt EP6 with an upgraded turbo kit often proves more cost-effective than repeated turbo repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EP6 THP rebuilt engines for your Peugeot 208

problems.h4: Diesel EGR & DPF Failure - Limp Mode & Power Loss
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration, causing soot buildup; EGR valves clog with carbon, restricting airflow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean EGR valve, force DPF regeneration via diagnostic tool
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; requires regular motorway driving to prevent recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned DPF unit, recalibrate pressure sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine is otherwise healthy; does not address EGR root cause

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV-series engine with updated EGR/DPF components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable for high-mileage diesels; resolves multiple system failures simultaneously

problems.recommendation: If your diesel 208 is used primarily for short trips, regular EGR cleaning and occasional forced DPF regeneration can extend component life. However, once the DPF is physically damaged or the EGR valve is seized, a rebuilt engine with updated emissions components often offers better long-term value than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV5 BlueHDi engine prices for your Peugeot 208

problems.h4: Piston Ring Wear - Excessive Oil Consumption on PureTech
problems.rootCause: Piston rings wear or stick, allowing oil to enter the combustion chamber, causing blue smoke, fouled spark plugs, and eventual catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace spark plugs, clean intake, use oil additive to free stuck rings
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary measure only; does not address physical ring wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings, decarbonise combustion chambers
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if cylinder bores are undamaged; requires precision machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt PureTech with upgraded piston rings, updated oil control, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; eliminates oil consumption and protects catalytic converter

problems.recommendation: If your PureTech is consuming over 1 litre of oil per 1,000 miles, ring replacement is rarely cost-effective on a 208. A rebuilt engine with upgraded ring design and oil control components typically offers better value and peace of mind, especially on vehicles worth £3,000+.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt PureTech engines with upgraded piston rings for your Peugeot 208

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - EP3 VTi Engine Rattle
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner loses pressure, allowing the chain to stretch and rattle, potentially causing valve timing errors or catastrophic interference failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner, reset timing, test for noise
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable only if chain and guides show minimal wear; early intervention key

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, sprockets; reset variable timing
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if rattling is audible; prevents valve damage if caught early

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EP3 with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if valve damage has occurred or engine has high mileage

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold start from your 1.2 VTi, act immediately - continued driving risks valve damage. For engines over 90k miles, a rebuilt EP3 with an upgraded timing kit often represents better value than a full timing service on an aged engine.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP3 VTi engine prices for your Peugeot 208

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 208
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing PureTech wet belt debris, EP6 turbo failure, or diesel EGR/DPF issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 208 specialists. Every rebuilt and reconditioned engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 208 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 208 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 208 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L HDi (DV4) was the entry diesel for early Mk1 208 models, offering strong economy but requiring regular EGR maintenance. Best suited to urban drivers who can manage DPF regeneration cycles.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 208 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The modern 1.5L BlueHDi (DV5) replaced the 1.6 HDi in Mk2 208, featuring SCR/AdBlue for Euro 6 compliance and improved refinement. The most common diesel replacement enquiry for 2019+ models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 208 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDi/BlueHDi (DV6) powered most Mk1 diesel 208 variants, offering a balance of performance and economy. High-mileage examples often require DPF/EGR system attention.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 208 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L PureTech (EB0) was offered in entry-level Mk1 208 trims, providing adequate performance for city driving with lower running costs than larger units.

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech (EB2) is the most common petrol engine in the 208 range, but its wet belt design requires proactive monitoring to prevent catastrophic failure. Dominates replacement enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: Wet belt degradation causing oil contamination

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.2L VTi Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 1.2L VTi (EP3) preceded the PureTech in early Mk1 208 models. Simpler design but timing chain tensioner wear can cause rattling and potential valve damage.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 208 1.2L VTi petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L VTi (EP5) was a mid-range petrol option for early Mk1 208, offering more power than the 1.2L without turbo complexity. Less common but still supported by our network.

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L VTi (EC5) and turbocharged THP (EP6) powered performance-oriented 208 variants. The EP6 THP is particularly sought-after for rebuilds due to turbo and timing chain concerns.

items.title: Peugeot 208 1.2L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech hybrid (HNR) pairs the EB2 engine with a 48V starter-generator for mild-hybrid efficiency. Specialist rebuilds required to maintain battery system compatibility. engineYears.h2: Peugeot 208 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 208's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Mk1 Launch: EP3 Petrol & DV4 Diesel Debut
years.description: The original Peugeot 208 launched with naturally aspirated petrol and entry diesel options, setting the foundation for the model's engine range.
keyChanges: Mk1 208 platform introduced with 3-door and 5-door body styles
EP3 1.2L VTi and EC5 1.6L VTi petrol engines available
DV4 1.4L HDi diesel launched as the sole diesel option
Entry trims: Access, Active; mid: Allure; top: Feline
knownFor: Simple, reliable naturally aspirated petrol engines
DV4 diesel prone to EGR clogging on urban use
Timing chain tensioner wear on EP3 after 80k miles
Strong residual values for well-maintained early examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2013 Peugeot 208 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 208 1.2 VTi engine replacement • Peugeot 208 1.4 HDi EGR problem • Peugeot 208 EP3 timing chain rattle

years.preview: PureTech Introduction: EB2 Wet Belt Era Begins
years.description: Peugeot introduced the new 1.2L PureTech (EB2) engine family, bringing turbo efficiency but also the wet belt design that would dominate replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: EB2 1.2L PureTech engines launched (68/82/100hp outputs)
Wet timing belt design introduced - requires proactive monitoring
DV6 1.6L HDi added to diesel range for more power
New trims: Style, Signature; GT Line performance variant introduced
knownFor: PureTech wet belt degradation starts appearing at 50k+ miles
DV6 diesel offers strong torque but DPF maintenance critical
EB2ADT becomes the most searched engine code for replacement
GT Line popularity drives EP6 THP enquiry growth
years.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2 PureTech engine prices for your Peugeot 208
years.ticker: Peugeot 208 PureTech wet belt failure • Peugeot 208 EB2ADT engine replacement • Peugeot 208 1.6 HDi DPF problem

years.preview: Mk1 Maturation: DV5 Diesel & EB2 Refinements
years.description: Mid-life updates brought the new 1.5L BlueHDi diesel and refined PureTech calibrations, while GTi performance variants kept enthusiast interest high.
keyChanges: DV5 1.5L BlueHDi introduced to replace DV6 in some trims
PureTech engines receive software updates for refinement
GTi by Peugeot Sport launched with 200hp EP6FDT variant
Black Edition and Tech Edition cosmetic packs added
knownFor: DV5 BlueHDi offers improved emissions but AdBlue system complexity
PureTech wet belt concerns now widely documented in owner forums
EP6FDT GTi engines sought-after for specialist turbo rebuilds
High demand for EB2ADT rebuilt units with upgraded belt kits
years.cta: Find rebuilt DV5 BlueHDi engines for your Peugeot 208
years.ticker: Peugeot 208 1.5 BlueHDi AdBlue fault • Peugeot 208 GTi turbo rebuild • Peugeot 208 PureTech oil consumption

years.preview: Mk2 Launch: New Platform, EB2 Dominance
years.description: The all-new Mk2 208 platform arrived with updated styling and electronics, but retained the EB2 PureTech and introduced mild-hybrid options.
keyChanges: All-new Mk2 platform with sharper styling and digital cockpit
EB2 PureTech remains the core petrol engine (68/100/130hp)
DV5 1.5L BlueHDi becomes the sole diesel offering
First mild-hybrid variant launched with 48V starter-generator
knownFor: Mk2 PureTech still uses wet belt - failure pattern continues
Hybrid system adds complexity but improves urban efficiency
DV5 BlueHDi reliability improved over DV6 but AdBlue faults persist
Highest volume of replacement enquiries now for 2019+ EB2ADT units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2020 Peugeot 208 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 208 Mk2 PureTech engine • Peugeot 208 Hybrid 100 replacement • Peugeot 208 1.5 BlueHDi injector fault

years.preview: Mk2 Updates: Hybrid Expansion & PureTech Refinements
years.description: Later Mk2 updates expanded hybrid availability and refined PureTech calibrations, while wet belt concerns remain the dominant replacement driver.
keyChanges: Hybrid variant expanded across more trim levels
PureTech engines receive updated belt material specifications
Facelift styling updates for 2023 model year
Continued focus on EB2 and DV5 as core powertrains
knownFor: Updated PureTech belt specs may extend service life but monitoring still critical
Hybrid battery health becomes a factor in high-mileage replacement decisions
DV5 BlueHDi remains the most reliable diesel option in the range
Strong demand for rebuilt EB2DTS with upgraded timing components
years.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2DTS PureTech 130 engine prices for your Peugeot 208
years.ticker: Peugeot 208 2023 facelift engine • Peugeot 208 PureTech belt update • Peugeot 208 Hybrid battery check

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Peugeot 208 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Peugeot 208 specialists.

faq.h2: Peugeot 208 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 208 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Peugeot 208 engine replacement costs range from £495 for a used unit to £2,500+ for a reconditioned engine, depending on engine code, condition and whether supply & fit is included.
keyPoints: Used engines start from £495 but carry higher risk
Rebuilt/reconditioned units: £1,700-£2,500 typical range
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 to engine price
PureTech and THP engines cost more due to complexity
items.cta: Get instant Peugeot 208 engine quotes from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot 208?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 208's compatible engine codes depend on year and trim - common codes include EB2ADT (1.2L PureTech), DV5RD (1.5L BlueHDi), and EP6DT (1.6L THP).
keyPoints: Enter your reg or engine code for instant compatibility check
EB2 series covers most 2014+ petrol 208 models
DV4/DV5/DV6 codes apply to diesel variants
Hybrid models use HNR code with e-DSC6 transmission
Never fit an engine without verifying the exact code match
Our DVLA lookup tool confirms compatibility in seconds
items.cta: Use our free Peugeot 208 engine code checker now

items.question: Is a reconditioned Peugeot 208 engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes - OEM-spec reconditioned Peugeot 208 engines from our network include upgraded components addressing known failures, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units replace wear items: bearings, seals, gaskets
PureTech engines receive upgraded wet belt kits
All engines bench-tested before dispatch
Warranty claims handled via UK fitting garages
Choose suppliers with proven Peugeot 208 experience
Request compression test reports before purchase
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Peugeot 208 engine suppliers today

items.question: How long does Peugeot 208 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines can be delivered next-day if in stock; supply & fit typically takes 48-72 hours from order to collection, depending on garage availability.
keyPoints: Stock engines: next-day delivery possible
Built-to-order rebuilds: 3-5 working days typical
Supply & fit booking depends on local garage slots
Hybrid/diesel units may require longer diagnostic time
Book early to secure your preferred fitting date
Confirm turnaround time with your chosen supplier
items.cta: Find Peugeot 208 engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: Can I replace my Peugeot 208 PureTech engine myself?
items.answer: Not recommended - PureTech engine replacement requires specialist tools, calibration equipment and hybrid system knowledge for MHEV variants.
keyPoints: EB2 engines need torque-to-yield bolt procedures
Timing belt alignment requires precision tools
Hybrid variants need high-voltage safety protocols
Incorrect installation voids warranty and risks damage
items.cta: Use our network of vetted Peugeot 208 engine fitters instead

items.question: What causes Peugeot 208 PureTech engine failure?
items.answer: Wet timing belt degradation is the primary cause - debris blocks the oil pickup, starving bearings and causing seizure, often between 50k-80k miles.
keyPoints: Belt material breaks down, contaminating oil system
Oil starvation leads to bearing failure and knock
Piston ring wear compounds oil consumption issues
Rebuilt engines include upgraded belt kits to prevent recurrence
Monitor oil level and condition regularly
Replace wet belt proactively before 60k miles if possible
items.cta: Get quotes for PureTech engines with upgraded belt systems

items.question: Do Peugeot 208 replacement engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with 3/6-month options also available at lower cost.
keyPoints: 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Warranty valid at any UK garage, not just the supplier
Claims processed through the fitting garage for convenience
Terms vary - always confirm coverage details before ordering
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Use recommended oil specs to avoid warranty disputes
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 208 engine warranty options from UK suppliers

items.question: How do I find my Peugeot 208 engine code?
items.answer: Check your V5C logbook (section V.5), the engine bay sticker near the strut tower, or use our free registration lookup tool for instant code identification.
keyPoints: V5C logbook lists engine code in section V.5
Engine bay sticker shows code near chassis number
Registration lookup pulls DVLA data instantly
Common codes: EB2ADT, DV5RD, EP6DT, HNR
Code format is usually 4-5 letters/numbers (e.g., HNK)
Always verify code before ordering a replacement engine
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 208 engine code in 60 seconds

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot 208 engine?
items.answer: Often yes - a rebuilt engine from £1,700 can extend your 208's life by years, versus £15k+ for a new car, especially if the rest of the vehicle is in good condition.
keyPoints: Compare engine cost against vehicle market value
Factor in remaining MOT, tyres, and service history
Rebuilt engines with warranty offer peace of mind
Supply & fit option removes DIY installation risk
Get multiple quotes to ensure competitive pricing
Consider selling the old engine/core for credit
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 208 engine replacement vs buying another car

items.question: Can I upgrade my Peugeot 208 engine during replacement?
items.answer: Yes - PureTech 100hp to 130hp swaps are possible with ECU recalibration, while diesel-to-hybrid conversions require specialist assessment for compatibility.
keyPoints: EB2ADT to EB2DTS upgrade needs ECU and turbo changes
Hybrid conversions require battery system integration
Always verify transmission compatibility first
Specialist suppliers can advise on feasible upgrades
Upgrades may affect insurance and MOT classification
Confirm all ancillary components are compatible before ordering
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 208 engine upgrade options with our specialists
